summary: This lecture discusses the nature and purpose of studying Sunnah through hadith collections, specifically contrasting books of Sunnah with books of Islamic jurisprudence (fiqh). The speaker explains that while scholars of different legal schools (madhabs) may differ on categorization and details, the vast majority of Islamic practices are agreed upon. The lecture emphasizes that proper Islamic knowledge requires learning through qualified scholars rather than attempting to understand complex theological topics through surface-level research, comparing this to how one cannot learn engineering without foundational mathematics.
suggested_title: Understanding Sunnah Collections and the Importance of Traditional Islamic Learning
catchy_title: Why You Cannot Google Your Way to Islamic Knowledge
